parameters.content: ## Auto‑Generate Release Notes for Any GitHub Repo → Slack

#🚀 GitHub Automated Release Workflow (n8n)

This workflow auto-generates release notes from merged pull requests and creates a draft GitHub release, notifying via Slack. Below are the descriptions for each node involved:

⸻

🧩 1. GitHub Release Input Form
	•	Purpose: Collects user input for:
	•	owner: GitHub username/org
	•	repo: Repository name
	•	branch: Target base branch

⸻

⚙️ 2. Config
	•	Purpose:
	•	Extracts user input from the form node.
	•	Defines the label grouping map to classify PRs under sections like feature, bug, performance, etc.

⸻

🔖 3. Get Latest Git Tag
	•	Purpose: Fetches the most recent semantic Git tag from the selected repository.
	•	Why: Used to determine what PRs were merged after the last release.

⸻

🕒 4. Get Commit Date of Latest Tag
	•	Purpose: Retrieves the commit timestamp for the latest tag.
	•	Why: This timestamp is used to filter only the PRs merged after the last release.

⸻

📥 5. Fetch All Merged PRs Since Last Tag
	•	Purpose: Queries GitHub’s Search API for pull requests:
	•	That are merged.
	•	Merged after the latest tag’s commit date.
	•	On the selected base branch.
	•	Why: Ensures only new, relevant PRs are included in the release.

⸻

🧠 6. Adjusted: Group PRs & Generate Release Note
	•	Purpose:
	•	Groups the filtered PRs by label (using Config map).
	•	Formats them into a clean Markdown release note.
	•	Adds a ✅ QA Checklist section at the end.

⸻

🪄 7. GitHub Pre Release
	•	Purpose:
	•	Creates a draft GitHub release.
	•	Uses the latest tag and generated notes.
	•	Sets:
	•	"draft": true
	•	"prerelease": false

⸻

💬 8. Send Message to Slack
	•	Purpose: Posts the release summary to a designated Slack channel.
	•	Includes: Tag name, categorized release notes, and optionally a link to the GitHub release.
